checks.note: Metric, baseline (4), target (0), horizon (next feature run, issue #64) and evidence source all named; the inspection procedure (round-directory manual-precheck-note.md files + citation check) is collectible. Minor residual judgment in deciding whether a note 'cites the documented procedure' (paraphrase vs verbatim), bounded and non-blocking.
One AGENTS.md subsection uniformly covering all 4 symptomatic phases matches the single structural root cause (inconsistent precheck contracts). Neither over- nor under-scoped.

AGENTS.md has no existing fallback/deviation provision to conflict with. The proposed fallback has explicit containment: applies only while the upstream defect is open, requires recorded human approval, preserves identity-ledger reservation parity, and requires citing issue #61 — so it cannot normalize bypassing a working precheck and preserves evidence-chain integrity. Minor: no specified mechanism/owner for retiring the subsection once #61 closes (staleness risk).
Single addition to a single existing file, writable in one commit; matches the Rollback-Plan's stated blast radius.

Scanned Root Cause Hypothesis, Change Description, and Expected Effect against every Section 2 forbidden term — none found; only required generic substitutions used. No newly introduced forbidden term from the Cycle 1 revision.
Revert-by-commit-message (WFI-002) is executable and complete; the claimed single-file scope matches the Proposed Change table.

findings.description: The fallback subsection is scoped 'only while the upstream precheck defect is open' but no mechanism or owner is specified for retiring the subsection once issue #61 closes — modest staleness risk.
Whether a manual-precheck-note.md 'cites the documented AGENTS.md procedure' involves a small reviewer judgment (paraphrase vs verbatim not defined); does not block executability.
